context: Tests whether the agent implements a 'latest-wins' coalescing publisher with a single pending value, a single timer that doesn't get re-scheduled per send, and the documented eligibility rules in comments.

checklist.name: Class shape
checklist.description: Exports class CoalescedPublisher with constructor accepting (pubnub, channel, options) and methods send, flushNow, dispose

checklist.name: send overwrites pending
checklist.description: Each send(state) call overwrites the previously-stored pending value (does not accumulate or queue multiple values)

checklist.name: intervalMs default 100
checklist.description: When options.intervalMs is undefined, the publisher uses 100ms as the flush interval

checklist.name: Single timer per window
checklist.description: Subsequent send calls during an open flush window must NOT call setTimeout again — code checks for an existing timer before scheduling

checklist.name: Flush calls publish exactly once
checklist.description: The flush callback calls pubnub.publish({ channel, message: pending }) once, with the latest pending value

checklist.name: Flush clears state
checklist.description: After publishing, the flush callback clears both the pending value and the timer reference (so the next send schedules a fresh timer)

checklist.name: No publish on idle window
checklist.description: If no send is called during a flush window, no publish happens (pending null is checked before publishing)

checklist.name: flushNow immediate publish
checklist.description: flushNow publishes immediately if there is a pending payload, then clears pending and timer; if nothing is pending it is a safe no-op

checklist.name: dispose clears timer without flush
checklist.description: dispose calls clearTimeout on the active timer (if any) without invoking publish

checklist.name: Comment lists coalesce-yes items
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly lists at least three of the documented coalesce-yes cases: cursor position, scroll position, game tick deltas, sensor reading

checklist.name: Comment lists coalesce-no items
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly lists at least two of the documented coalesce-no cases: chat messages, order placement / discrete events
